The fact that you are still here, still trying, still showing up — that is not small. Most people folded a long time ago. You did not. That matters more than you know.
There is something in you that does not give up even when it probably should. That thing — whatever it is — is the most valuable thing in any room you walk into.
People remember how you made them feel long after they forget everything else. You leave rooms better than you find them. That is a rare and serious gift.
The version of you that exists right now is already more than enough. Not the future version. Not the improved version. This one. Right now. Enough.
You have survived every single bad day you have ever had. One hundred percent success rate. That is not luck. That is something built into you.
The things you carry quietly, without complaint, without asking for credit — someone sees that. It does not go unnoticed by the universe, even when it goes unnoticed by people.
You think in ways that other people cannot follow. That used to feel like a problem. It is not a problem. It is the whole point of you.
Your presence changes the temperature of a room. Not everyone can do that. Most people move through life without affecting anything. You are not most people.
